title: Itchy Skin? Try These 10 Soothing Herbal Teas
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy skin are a natural remedy that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the discomfort and inflammation associated with itchy skin conditions. The best herbal teas for itchy skin include Urtica dioica, Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe vera, Taraxacum officinale, Symphytum officinale, Althaea officinalis, Chamomilla recutita, and Echinacea purpurea. If left untreated, itchy skin can lead to anxiety, sleep disturbances, and social isolation, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea, also known as nettle tea, is a natural remedy that can help soothe itchy skin. The leaves of the nettle plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and itching caused by allergic reactions. When you drink nettle tea, the compounds are absorbed into your bloodstream and can travel to the affected area, where they help to calm the skin. The tea also contains antioxidants that can help protect the skin from further damage. In addition, nettle tea can help to reduce the release of histamine, a chemical that causes itching and swelling. By reducing histamine levels, nettle tea can provide quick relief from itchy skin. Nettle tea is often used to treat skin conditions like eczema, hives, and insect bites.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation, soothing and calming the skin.

Tannins are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and their astringent qualities help to dry out the affected area and soothe the skin.

Polyphenols, commonly known as dandelion, are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness and promoting healthy skin healing.
Isorhapontigenin is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help to reduce the swelling and itching caused by skin irritations.

remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal teas for itchy skin
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal teas for itchy skin, start by gathering fresh dandelion flowers or roots, which are the key ingredients. If using flowers, pick them from a clean area, and make sure they are free of any dirt or pollutants. Next, dry the flowers or roots completely, either by air-drying them or using a low-temperature oven. Once they are dry, crush them into small pieces to release their medicinal properties. Now, combine one teaspoon of the crushed dandelion flowers or roots with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active ingredients to infuse into the water. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea into a separate container to remove any loose particles. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but make sure to consume the tea while it is still warm, as the heat from the tea can help to soothe itchy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is a great remedy for itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tea contains a compound called triterpenoids, which helps reduce swelling and redness in the skin. This makes it easier to soothe itchy skin and relieve discomfort. Calendula officinalis tea also has antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infections that can cause further irritation. When applied topically, the tea can help calm the skin and reduce the urge to scratch. It's also rich in antioxidants, which protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This can help promote healthy skin and reduce the appearance of itchiness.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin, also known as dandelion, is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritation, making it a natural remedy for skin problems.
Saponins are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the skin, providing relief from itchy and irritated skin conditions.
Flavonoids, also known as dandelion, are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and soothe irritated skin.
Kaempferol is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and itching by blocking the release of chemical messengers that trigger allergic reactions.
Tannins, also known as Dandelion, can help itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, and also have antihistamine effects that stop the itchiness.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for itchy skin
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal tea that helps with itchy skin, start by gathering fresh dandelion leaves or roots from a trusted source. Wash the leaves or roots thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, dry the leaves or roots in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for 2-3 hours, or air dry them for a few days. Once the leaves or roots are dry, measure out 1-2 teaspoons of them and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Pour in 8 ounces of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup and add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe itchy skin. Note: It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is good for itchy skin because it has many helpful ingredients. Aloe vera, the main ingredient in this tea, has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and redness on the skin, making it less itchy. The tea also contains antioxidants, which fight against bad cells in the body that can cause skin problems. Aloe vera has a soothing effect on the skin, calming it down and making it feel better. When you drink the tea or apply it to the skin, the anti-inflammatory and soothing properties help to ease the itchiness, making it a good natural remedy for itchy skin. The tea also has antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infections that can make itchy skin worse.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids, also known as dandelion, are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the discomfort and itching caused by conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
Aloin has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itchy skin by reducing swelling and redness, providing relief from discomfort and irritation.

Anthraquinones are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm and reduce redness and irritation, providing relief from itchy and uncomfortable skin conditions.
Aloe-emodin is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for itchy skin
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal tea that helps with itchy skin, start by gathering dandelion roots or leaves from a trusted source. You will need about one teaspoon of dried dandelion root or two teaspoons of fresh leaves for every cup of boiling water. If using fresh leaves, make sure to wash them thoroughly and pat them dry before using. Next, combine the dandelion root or leaves with boiling water in a saucepan or teapot and let it steep for about 5-10 minutes, depending on the desired strength of the tea. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. You can add a bit of honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to avoid adding any other ingredients that might interfere with the tea's medicinal properties. Once the tea has cooled, you can drink it as needed to help soothe itchy skin. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups a day for optimal benefits, but always consult with a healthcare professional before using any new herbal remedy, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tea, also known as St. John's Wort tea, is a herbal remedy that's good for itchy skin. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness in the skin. The tea contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin that calm the skin and ease irritation. It's also antiseptic, which means it helps prevent infection in the affected area. When you drink the tea, the compounds are absorbed into your bloodstream and then travel to your skin, where they can help soothe itchy patches. Some people use it to treat skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis, which cause itchy skin. The tea may also help reduce the itching and discomfort caused by bug bites or minor scrapes.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the skin, providing relief from itching and irritation.

Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy skin by reducing redness and swelling.

remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal teas for itchy skin
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal tea, which can help with itchy skin, start by gathering a handful of dried dandelion flowers or roots. If you're using fresh dandelion flowers, simply dry them by tying them in a bunch and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Next, combine 1 teaspoon of the dried dandelion flowers or roots with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled slightly. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, and discard the solids. You can now drink the tea as is, or add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and a bit of honey to taste. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day to help alleviate itchy skin, and to consider consulting with a healthcare professional before using the tea if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea has been used for years to help with itchy skin. This tea comes from a plant called coneflower, which is rich in special helpers called flavonoids and phenolic acids. These helpers have strong anti-inflammatory properties, which mean they can reduce swelling and redness in the skin. When you drink Echinacea purpurea tea, the flavonoids and phenolic acids are absorbed into your body, where they can reach the skin and calm down itchiness. The tea also contains other compounds that can help soothe the skin and reduce irritation. People who try Echinacea purpurea tea often report feeling relief from itchy skin, especially when it's caused by allergies or bug bites. The tea is thought to work by reducing the amount of histamine in the body, which is a chemical that can make skin itch and get red. By reducing histamine, Echinacea purpurea tea can help calm down itchy skin and make it feel more comfortable.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal tea that helps with itchy skin, you will need dried dandelion roots and leaves. First, boil 1 quart of water in a large pot, then add 2-3 tablespoons of dried dandelion roots and leaves to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties into the water. Once the steeping time is complete, remove the tea infuser or cup from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Strain the tea into a separate container to remove the solids, and discard the solids. You can now drink the Taraxacum officinale herbal tea hot or chilled, sweetened with honey if desired. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day to help soothe and calm itchy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe itchy skin. The tea is made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, which contains special compounds that calm and cool the skin. When you drink aloe vera tea, these compounds are absorbed into your body and can help reduce inflammation and itching. This is especially helpful for people with skin conditions like eczema or psoriasis, who often experience itchy skin. Aloe vera tea can also help to lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and soft. Additionally, the tea may help to reduce redness and irritation, making it a great option for people who want a natural way to manage itchy skin. Drinking aloe vera tea can provide quick relief from itchy skin, and it can be used as part of a larger skincare routine to keep skin healthy and balanced.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata tea, made from the leaves of the plant, is a natural remedy for itchy skin. The leaves contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and redness on the skin. This can provide relief from itchy skin caused by allergies, insect bites, or skin conditions like eczema. The tea also has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This can help to soothe and calm itchy skin, reducing the urge to scratch and further irritation. Additionally, Plantago lanceolata tea has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like acne, dermatitis, and psoriasis, and its anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce the severity of these conditions.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal teas that help with itchy skin, you will need fresh or dried dandelion root and leaves, a large pot, a tea strainer or infuser, and a cup. First, rinse the dandelion roots and leaves with water, then chop them into small pieces. Next, combine the chopped dandelion roots and leaves with two cups of water in the large pot, and bring the mixture to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through the tea strainer or infuser into your cup, making sure to separate the dandelion solids from the liquid. You can drink the Taraxacum officinale herbal tea hot or cold, sweetened with honey if desired. As you drink the tea, the anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties of the dandelion may help to soothe and calm itchy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tea, also known as comfrey tea, is a natural remedy that can help soothe itchy skin. The plant contains a compound called allantoin, which helps to calm and protect the skin. When applied topically, comfrey tea can reduce inflammation and itching caused by conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and insect bites. The anti-inflammatory properties in comfrey tea can also help to reduce redness and swelling. Additionally, comfrey tea has been used to promote skin regeneration and healing, which can help to repair damaged skin tissue and reduce the appearance of scars. Drinking comfrey tea may also help to promote overall health and well-being, which can contribute to healthier skin. Comfrey tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help to neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from damage caused by environmental stressors.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal tea for itchy skin, start by gathering fresh dandelion leaves and flowers. You can grow your own or buy them from a health food store. Rinse the leaves and flowers with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried dandelion leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup using a cheesecloth or a tea strainer. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use a small amount as the tea is quite potent. Once the tea has cooled down, you can apply it to the itchy skin using a cotton ball or a cloth. Alternatively, you can drink the tea internally to help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations from the inside out. It's essential to note that if your itchy skin persists or worsens, you should consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis tea, also known as marshmallow root tea, is a natural remedy for itchy skin. The roots of the marshmallow plant contain a thick, slimy liquid called mucilage, which is responsible for its soothing effects. When you drink the tea or apply it to the skin, the mucilage forms a protective barrier that helps to lock in moisture and calm the skin. This can be especially helpful for people with eczema, psoriasis, or other skin conditions that cause dryness and irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties of Althaea officinalis tea may also help to reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to manage itchy skin. Furthermore, the tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing inflammation and soothing the skin.

The tea is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm irritated skin. It also contains taraxasterol, a compound that has been shown to have anti-itch properties. When applied topically, the tea can help reduce inflammation and itching caused by skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. The antioxidants in the tea can also help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to skin irritation. Drinking the tea may also help to reduce inflammation from the inside out, which can be beneficial for people who experience itchy skin due to underlying health conditions. The tea is also gentle and non-irritating, making it a good option for people with sensitive skin.

remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal teas for itchy skin
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Taraxacum officinale herbal tea to help with itchy skin, start by gathering fresh dandelion leaves and roots from a trusted source. Clean the leaves and roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, chop the leaves and roots into small pieces and put them in a pot with 2 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 5-10 minutes. After the tea has cooled down, strain it into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids. You can drink the tea straight, or add honey and lemon to taste. For itchy skin, it's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally before meals. Some people also like to use the cooled tea as a compress or a bath soak to help soothe itchy skin. Remember to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies.

supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal teas to remedy itchy skin is a blend of 'Urtica dioica', 'Calendula officinalis', and 'Althaea officinalis'. 'Urtica dioica', also known as nettle tea, has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe itchy skin. 'Calendula officinalis', or calendula tea, is rich in flavonoids that reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. 'Althaea officinalis', or marshmallow root tea, has a soothing effect on the skin due to its mucilaginous properties, which help calm irritation and itching. This combination works well together because it addresses the skin issue from multiple angles. The anti-inflammatory properties of nettle tea and calendula tea help reduce redness and swelling, while the soothing effect of marshmallow root tea calms the skin, providing relief from itching and irritation.
supplementary_avoid: If you have itchy skin, you may want to avoid certain herbal teas. For example, tea made from the leaves of the Stinging Nettle tree (Cirrus spp.) can cause skin irritation in some people. This is because it contains compounds that can increase inflammation and make itching worse. Another tea to be cautious about is chamomile tea, which can sometimes cause skin reactions in people with sensitive skin. Chamomile contains chemicals that can cause an allergic reaction, leading to itching and redness. Additionally, tea made from the leaves of the sage plant (Salvia officinalis) can be too drying for the skin, making it itch even more. Sage contains compounds that can reduce moisture in the skin, leading to dryness and irritation. Finally, tea made from the leaves of the dandelion plant (Taraxacum officinalis, though you mentioned you wouldn't include this one - I did, but I will leave this one out, I will instead say) tea made from the leaves of the goldenseal plant (Hydrastis canadensis) contains chemicals that can cause skin irritation and make itching worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help soothe itchy skin. It contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling. When applied to the skin, the gel can create a protective barrier that locks in moisture and helps calm irritated skin. This makes it ideal for skin conditions like eczema and sunburn.  Oatmeal baths are another natural remedy for itchy skin. The starch in oatmeal can help reduce inflammation and itching, while its anti-irritant properties can calm the skin. When added to bath water, oatmeal can create a soothing and comforting experience that can provide relief from itchy skin. This is especially beneficial for people with skin conditions like eczema and atopic dermatitis.  Apple cider vinegar has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy skin. When applied to the skin, it can create a pH balance that can help reduce itching and inflammation. Apple cider vinegar can also help to prevent infections that can cause itchiness and redness.  Coconut oil is another natural remedy that can help with itchy skin. It contains fatty acids like lauric acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce itching and inflammation. When applied to the skin, coconut oil can create a barrier that locks in moisture and helps to soothe irritated skin.  Epsom salt baths can also provide relief from itchy skin. The magnesium in Epsom salt can help to relax the body and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to itchy skin. When added to bath water, Epsom salt can create a soothing and calming experience that can provide relief from itchy skin.  Baking soda can help to neutralize skin pH and reduce itching and inflammation. When mixed with water to create a paste, baking soda can be applied to itchy skin to provide relief.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have itchy skin often experience other ailments like eczema, allergies, and digestive issues. Eczema can be caused by an imbalance of good and bad bacteria on the skin, which can be treated with medicinal herbs like aloe vera and calendula. Aloe vera soothes and calms the skin, while calendula reduces inflammation.  Allergies, especially skin allergies, can be linked to itchy skin. Herbs like quercetin, found in plants like chamomile and willow bark, can help alleviate allergy symptoms. Quercetin has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and itching.  Digestive issues like constipation, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) can also cause itchy skin. This is often due to the buildup of toxins in the body, which can be treated with herbs like dandelion root and milk thistle. Dandelion root stimulates digestion and removes toxins, while milk thistle protects the liver from damage.  Herbs like oat straw and licorice root can also help soothe itchy skin caused by digestive issues. Oat straw reduces inflammation and calms the digestive system, while licorice root protects the gut lining and reduces inflammation.  Some people with itchy skin may experience anxiety and stress, which can be treated with herbs like passionflower and lemon balm. Passionflower calms the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, while lemon balm soothes and calms the mind and body.
